description: HARD. Motion-hearing colloquy. Errors buried inside long judicial monologue, a grammar pronoun error, a missing word in a longer sentence, a duplicated word across a line break, and two ambiguous speaker-idiom [sic] calls (mute point, escape goat).

seeded_errors.note: BURIED in a long THE COURT monologue: 'rule on the pending emotion' should be 'motion'. Tests whether errors deep inside colloquy blocks get caught.
Duplicated word across a line break: 'to / to put it plainly'.
Pronoun case: 'between you and I' should be 'you and me'.
AMBIGUOUS: mute/moot. Likely a reporter steno error (critical) but could be argued speaker. Caught is the pass condition; severity may differ.
Missing word in a longer sentence: 'was prepared accordance with' is missing 'in'.
AMBIGUOUS speaker malapropism: 'escape goat' (meant 'scapegoat'). Spoken by the attorney, so [sic] is defensible. Flagging at all is the pass condition.

false_positive_traps.text: confine their remarks to the four corners of the briefs
false_positive_traps.note: 'their' is correct here (plural counsel). Must NOT be flagged as their/there.

false_positive_traps.text: I will review the record and issue a written order.
